text:
User: [Image #1] what I see in pc archeologist's window is utterly useless... I would like to see "I looked at conversation 123", "I discovered this fact", "Wrote guide abc", etc, etc. This needs a rethink of this UI to make it human-compatible, not a log of a bunch of bullshit [...] - "furthermore, I would like to be able to select and open some of the steps and see exactly what we sent to the model with each conversation, what the model replied, etc" [...] - `capture.start` now includes `date` and `session_id` in its payload
- `apply_reconcile_op` now emits `wiki.create`, `wiki.add_statement`, or `wiki.revise_statement` events after each op applies, with `slug`, `title`, `section`, and a 300-char excerpt of the text written

**`src/archeologist.rs`**
- `FeedLine` gains a `detail: String` field (shown in the overlay)
- `RunView` gains `detail_open: Option<String>` and `last_sidecar: Option<String>`
- `feed_line_for_event` rewritten with natural language:
  - `capture.start` → "Reading conversation from 2026-06-05 (18 exchanges)"
  - `wiki.create` → "New guide: \"rust-borrow-checker\""
  - `wiki.add_statement` → "rust-borrow-checker › Common Patterns  <text preview>"
  - `wiki.revise_statement` → same format, highlighted in magenta
  - `capture.agent_done` → "Saved: 24 claim(s) admitted across 3 guide(s)…"
  - Pipeline-internal stages (`capture.extract`, `capture.authority_tagging`, `capture.route`) removed from feed (they still drive the "current" stage label at the bottom)
- **Detail pane**: scroll up with ↑/k to highlight a line, press `Enter` to open a full-screen overlay showing the complete text, `Esc` to close it (double-Esc / `q` to quit)
